article.title: Illuminating the dark spaces of healthcare with ambient intelligence

meta.title: Illuminating the dark spaces of healthcare with ambient intelligence

references.author: Y LeCun
references.unstructured: LeCun, Y., Bengio, Y. & Hinton, G. Deep learning. Nature 521, 436–444 (2015). This paper reviews developments in deep learning and explains common neural network architectures such as convolutional and recurrent neural networks when applied to visual and natural language-processing tasks.

references.unstructured: Jordan, M. I. & Mitchell, T. M. Machine learning: trends, perspectives, and prospects. Science 349, 255–260 (2015).

references.author: A Esteva
references.unstructured: Esteva, A. et al. A guide to deep learning in healthcare. Nat. Med. 25, 24–29 (2019). This perspective describes the use of computer vision, natural language processing, speech recognition and reinforcement learning for medical imaging tasks, electronic health record analysis, robotic-assisted surgery and genomic research.

references.author: EJ Topol
references.unstructured: Topol, E. J. High-performance medicine: the convergence of human and artificial intelligence. Nat. Med. 25, 44–56 (2019). This review outlines how artificial intelligence is used by clinicians, patients and health systems to interpret medical images, find workflow efficiencies and promote patient self-care.
